q: The aircraft was overloaded (1) / there was something wrong of the battery (2) / and the engine was making a queer noise. (3)
options: there was something wrong of the battery
and the engine was making a queer noise.
correction: wrong of the battery → wrong with the battery

q: I warned him (1) / for the danger (2) / he was going (3) / to face during the hiking. (4)
correction: for the danger → about the danger

q: Ordinarily, when in difficulty (1) / Renuka prefers keeping her counsel (2) / than running about (3) / taking advice. (4)
correction: than running about → rather than running about

q: Sheeila advised to (1) / her child not to (2) / play with the ball (3) / on the road. (4)
Basu was trying to pass (1) / through the gap on (2) / the fence when he (3) / tore his shirt. (4)
correction: through the gap on → through the gap in

q: I have been teaching in this school (1) / since several years (2) / but have never met (3) / such a hopeless class as this. (4)
options: I have been teaching in this school
such a hopeless class as this.
correction: since several years → for several years

q: It is easy to see that (1) / a lawyer's demeanour in court (2) / may be prejudicial against the interests of his client. (3)
options: It is easy to see that
may be prejudicial against the interests of his client.

q: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax (1) / is due to fact (2) / that we did not receive pay on time. (3)
options: The reason we have not been able to pay income tax
that we did not receive pay on time.
correction: due to fact → due to the fact

q: The policemen fired all the (1) / students when (2) / they were attacked (3) / by some of them. (4)
correction: fired all the students → fired at all the students

q: He was not promoted (1) / to the rank of colonel (2) / till for a few (3) / months of his resignation. (4)
correction: till for a few → until a few

q: All the players (1) / except Sam (2) / were present (3) / into the field. (4)
correction: into the field → on the field

q: Despite of a good monsoon (1) / this year, the production (2) / of food grains in the (3) / country did not go up. (4)
correction: Despite of a good monsoon → Despite a good monsoon

q: Yesterday, while (1) / crossing the road (2) / he was (3) / run out by a truck. (4)
correction: run out by → run over by

q: The girl wrote an essay (1) / so well (2) / that her teacher was exceedingly (3) / pleased at her. (4)
correction: pleased at her → pleased with her

q: He tried (1) / to open the cane (2) / by a cane opener. (3)
correction: to open the cane → to open the can
